text.title: Turborepo Task Pipeline Configuration
text.summary: turbo.json pipeline rules: dependsOn, outputs, env declarations, and cache correctness.

rule.statement: Every cacheable task must declare explicit `outputs`. Without outputs, Turborepo caches nothing useful and returns empty directories on cache hit.
rule.rationale: Turbo stores the file set listed in `outputs` as the cache artifact. An empty `outputs: []` stores nothing; subsequent runs fetch an empty result and rebuild silently.

rule.statement: Declare every environment variable that affects build output in `env` (per-task) or `globalEnv` (all tasks). Undeclared env vars cause stale cache hits across configurations.
rule.rationale: If `NODE_ENV` or `API_URL` changes the build output but is not listed, Turbo returns the cached prod artifact for a dev build — a silent correctness bug.

rule.statement: Mark `dev` and `start` tasks with `"cache": false, "persistent": true`. Never cache long-running processes.
rule.rationale: Dev servers are non-deterministic and never complete. Caching them stores incomplete state; `persistent: true` tells Turbo to keep the process alive indefinitely.

rule.statement: Use `"dependsOn": ["^build"]` (caret prefix) for tasks that need upstream packages built first. Use `"dependsOn": ["build"]` (no caret) for tasks that need the same package built first.
rule.rationale: The caret `^` means "all dependencies of this package must complete this task first." Without caret, typecheck might run before the upstream package emits its type declarations, causing false errors.

example.note: Turbo v2+ uses `tasks` key; v1 used `pipeline`. Always check the installed version before copy-pasting examples.
Missing dependsOn: typecheck may run before upstream packages emit declarations, producing false type errors.

section.title: Remote Cache

rule.statement: Set up remote cache from day one and enable signature verification (`"signature": true`) in shared environments.
rule.rationale: Without remote cache, every CI machine rebuilds from scratch. Without signature verification, a corrupt artifact uploaded by one machine breaks every consumer until manually purged.

section.reference: Reference turbo.json with correct pipeline, cache flags, and env declarations
